microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a 2-person operation into a **platformized system**, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led 0→1 development of an internal **Resilience Automation Platform**, defining self-service scheduling requirements that cut operational toil by 30% and transitioned end-to-end drill execution to a reusable, scalable model any team could turn on.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a **rack-level drill program** in 4 months with a 94% recovery rate — surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation standard across the org.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract requiring real-time coordination across engineering and ops teams.
Built an internal **AI agent** for drill planning that restructured end-to-end scheduling workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and expanding drill capacity by 21% without adding headcount.

Scoped and delivered partner integrations across Databricks, SAP, and NetApp, mapping each customer's real operational dependencies before pilot — shipping working integration slices that earned production sign-off and fed reusable platform components.

Championed the customer perspective inside Engineering and Product by synthesizing field findings from 14+ enterprise pilots into **roadmap input**, directly shaping the platform's self-service scheduling and feedback-loop instrumentation priorities.
microsoft_2023: Drove **product adoption** for AI-powered code generation workflows across Azure, running hands-on customer demos and training sessions that activated 14 key engineering teams and saved **37 hours monthly** in manual development effort.

Surfaced critical feature gaps by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams, translating messy real-world workflows into intent-based YAML generation requirements that were prioritized directly onto the product roadmap.

Improved data integrity and retrieval by migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% and establishing a reusable indexing pattern adopted across multiple service teams.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework spanning 140+ teams — turning one-off manual processes into a reusable, scalable deployment model.

Conducted discovery with **20+ service teams** to map 81 hours of manual toil per region launch, then built a product roadmap to automate critical paths and eliminate one-off custom work from the standard region bring-up process.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to target automation gaps, prioritize high-impact investments, and instrument downstream metrics flowing back into the platform roadmap.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and scoping integration end-to-end across IT, Operations, and Engineering teams.

Facilitated Agile sprint planning and retrospectives, **prioritizing 40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and unblock field-ops deployment teams relying on consistent software delivery across robotics stations.

Drove cross-functional alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to ship automated **CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and turning a previously manual release process into a reusable, production-grade workflow.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by managing end-to-end scoping and invoicing for 200+ monthly proposals, redesigning the CRM workflow to eliminate manual handoffs and create a reusable customer operations process across the sales team.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and lifted conversions 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy — optimizing the website, Google profile, and lead-capture funnel to bring real-world customer data back into pricing decisions.

Improved leadership **pricing models** by analyzing financial profitability across 1,000+ project records, identifying margin leaks and translating messy field-ops data into actionable product and operational changes for the estimating workflow.

tailoring_notes: Wove 'reusable integration pieces' / 'turn one-off custom work into reusable product' framing into microsoft_ft bullet#2, amazon_robotics bullet#3, and pro_painters bullet#1 — directly mirroring PermitFlow's core FDE expectation.

Used 'shipping working integration slices that earned production sign-off' in microsoft_ft bullet#6 and 'integration end-to-end' in amazon_robotics bullet#1 to mirror JD language: 'Comfortable owning an integration end to end' and 'Ship a working slice that earns the deal.'

Inserted 'map each customer's real operational dependencies before pilot' (microsoft_ft bullet#6) and 'messy real-world workflows' (microsoft_2023 bullet#2) to echo JD's 'Map how their permitting work really happens, not the demo version' and 'prototyping on messy, real-world data.'

Referenced 'production feedback loops' and 'downstream metrics flowing back into the platform' in microsoft_2022 bullet#3 and microsoft_ft bullet#7 — directly mirroring JD's 'Build production feedback loops. Confidence scores, human overrides, and downstream metrics flow back into the product.'

Mentioned Salesforce, Procore, and ServiceTitan in skills section and alluded to CRM/ERP integration experience (pro_painters CRM bullet, microsoft_ft partner integrations) to match JD's named tools and 'field-ops tools' language.
